final_answer: Bank of America's Series MM Preferred Stock (Fixed-to-Floating Rate Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series MM), issued in January 2020, carries a dividend rate of 4.300% per annum to, but excluding, January 28, 2025, and 3-month CME Term SOFR + 292.561 basis points thereafter. The per-share annual dividend (on the $25,000 liquidation preference) is $43.00. (The Series MM Preferred Stock was redeemed in whole by the Company on January 28, 2025.) Source: Bank of America 2024 annual report, Preferred Stock summary table on page 144, with additional details on pages 188 and 190.

selected_chunks.content: Series MM Fixed-to-Floating Rate Non-Cumulative January 2020 30,753 25,000 769  4.300% to, but excluding, 1/28/25; 3-mo. CME Term SOFR + 292.561 bps thereafter  43.00 33  On or after January 28, 2025

44,000 shares were designated as Fixed-to-Floating Rate Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series MM (the "Series MM Preferred Stock"), having a liquidation preference of $25,000 per share, 30,753 shares of which were issued and outstanding; The Series MM Preferred Stock was redeemed in whole by the Company on January 28, 2025.

As of their respective original issue dates, the terms of the Series E, F, G, AA, DD, FF, MM, 1, 2, 4 and 5 Preferred Stock (the "LIBOR Preferred Stock") provided for dividend rates to be determined by reference to three-month U.S. dollar LIBOR. Following LIBOR cessation, three-month CME Term SOFR plus a tenor spread adjustment of 0.26161% ("Adjusted Three-Month Term SOFR") replaced Three-Month USD LIBOR as the reference rate for dividend determinations.
